item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I’ve been reading through your list, and I like your ideas in all of them so far… but why is Dune so edgy?

He just strikes me as a throwback to the characters in the early wiki…. which while fun as an idea, really clashes with the sort of professionalism I expect from a doctor in the foundation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ohh cuz the… it's monkeys and the title is a gorillaz song

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*picks up gun*
Tales, hand them over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I simply love these hyper-dimensional nomad orangutans and their prophecy-poetry (also the article was very well written). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hyper dimensional god monkeys. Welp. That's a thing now. +1 in the hopes we might get a canon following on that gives us some insight into the devourer and maybe some further shenanigans in monkeh town

item.title: monky
item.content:encoded:
monky
monky

damned fine, Doctor Dune. Damned fine. Very good characterization, very nice execution, quite weird. once again, damned fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the margins have something to do with the theme I used. If you have a fix, let me know.

Ah, apologies. I meant the padding, not the margins — I always confuse those two somehow. It's fine as is.

item.title: Re: Interesting
item.content:encoded:
Headcanon time: Area-179 is pretty low on the Foundation's priority list. Safety > diplomacy, especially in the case of an entire population disappearing and the Foundation genuinely having no idea what they're up against. This doctor- stated in the article- has a knack for dealing with more dangerous objects, so why not send him? You've read other articles and see the stuff they deal with on a daily basis. This is a first encounter, and they're taking precautions because of the dangers that could be present when first dealing with an unknown anomaly.

I fixed the frequency as well. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the margins have something to do with the theme I used. If you have a fix, let me know.

They are meant to be em dashes, but I will try to use them less next article.

item.title: Interesting
item.content:encoded:
Although I really wonder why the Foundation would send someone so badly trained in diplomacy. He resorts to force right off the bat. Something about that seems kind of off.

Also, there's no such thing as a frequency of 200dB. It should be either "a frequency of 200Hz" or "an amplitude of 200dB" depending on what you meant. It should be noted however that a 200dB sound would probably kill most living organisms right where they stood, as well as cause a huge amount of collateral damage. That's almost as loud as a Space Shuttle launch as measured on the pad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this a lot but I take issue with the occasional excessive redaction/expungement. Additionally, there's just something kind of… "off" about what you've done with the blockquote margins.

Also, are the occasional hyphens at the end of words meant to be em dashes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It could be! Either way, whatever he was doing there wouldn't have had a good outcome for the monkeys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this.

Btw is this:

Dune traces a circle onto his palm, preparing to [DATA EXPUNGED]

A reference to SCP-4776 per chance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading the containment procedures, I really thought this was going to be an SCP version of the Random Chimp Event meme. +1

Edit: In Negotiations, you still have "where SCP-XXX-A-402 sits", you may want to change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well written and interesting- I like it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had fun writing this, and I hope you have fun reading it. That's all, really.

Image Credit

Location image made by me

Special thanks to Christopoulos for looking at this awhile back.
